text: During the laparoscopic appendectomy for suppurative acute appendicitis, pus is visible in the right paracolic gutter and pelvis. The appendix is amalgamated with the terminal ileum and cecum, forming an appendicular mass. Blunt dissection is performed to separate the appendix from the surrounding terminal ileum and cecum. The surgical field appears hyperemic. The surgeon uses a Maryland Dissector with electrocautery to dissect the mesoappendix, followed by a hook due to the toughness of the mesoappendix tissue.
The surgeon continues blunt dissection using the Maryland dissector to locate the base of the appendix. The surrounding tissues appear hyperemic and bloody as the dissection progresses.
The surgeon uses a grasper to hold the appendix and a knot pusher to slide a knot at the base of the appendix. The thread is cut, and the appendix is further separated.
The surgeon retrieves the appendix through the umbilical port using a 5mm laparoscopic lens, followed by irrigation and aspiration of the surgical field. A drain is then inserted.
